##NAMES=Stannite
##RRUFFID=R050187
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Cu_2_FeSnS_4_
##LOCALITY=Yaogangxian mine, Yizhang County, Hunan Province, China
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Marcus Origlieri and George Guanghua Liu
##DESCRIPTION=Bluish-black octahedron associated with fluorite and quartz. According to Kissin & Owens (1989), since 2a is significantly larger than c, this sample is stannite.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R050187
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Cu_2.00_(Fe_0.66_Zn_0.34_)Sn_1.00_S_4.00_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.455 b: 5.455 c: 10.777 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 320.72 crystal system: tetragonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
